title: NOT Gate (Inverter)
description: Output is ON when input is OFF, and OFF when input is ON. Core of all redstone logic — every complex circuit uses inversion.

steps.title: Foundation
steps.explanation: 3 stone blocks in a line. The leftmost holds the lever, middle carries signal, rightmost is the anchor block the torch attaches to.

steps.title: Input lever
steps.explanation: Place the lever on top of the first stone. This is your signal source — flip it to test the gate.

steps.title: Signal wire
steps.explanation: Two dust tiles carry the lever's signal east. The dust at x=2 sits on top of the anchor block and strongly powers it — that strong power is what the torch detects.

steps.title: Inverter torch
steps.explanation: Place a wall torch on the east face of the anchor block (the block at x=2). When the dust above strongly powers that block, this torch turns OFF — output inverted.

steps.title: Output lamp
steps.explanation: Place a redstone lamp directly east of the torch. The lamp lights when the torch is ON (lever OFF) and goes dark when the lever is flipped — that is inversion in action.
